step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with option B because cavity varnish primarily acts by reducing the permeability of acid into dentinal tubules—this is well-supported by evidence showing its ability to seal dentinal tubules and limit acid infiltration. While fluoride release plays a supportive role in remineralization, the core mechanism behind its effectiveness lies in creating a physical barrier that reduces acid penetration. Option A (thermal conductivity reduction) lacks direct evidence; option C (action as an obtundant) is incorrect since it has no analgesic properties. Therefore, B best captures the primary function of cavity varnish. Final answer: **B**. 

I agree with option B. Cavity varnish effectively reduces acid penetration into dentinal tubules by sealing them, thereby protecting against demineralization—the central mechanism supported by clinical studies. This makes B the most accurate and comprehensive choice among the options. Final answer: **B**.
